text_original: Dun indicates successful progress (in its circumstances). To a small extent it will (still) be advantageous to be firm and correct.
text_normalized: Dun indicates successful progress (in its circumstances). To a small extent it will (still) be advantageous to be firm and correct.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=Judgment.

text_original: The first SIX, divided, shows a retiring tail. The position is perilous. No movement in any direction should be made.
text_normalized: The first SIX, divided, shows a retiring tail. The position is perilous. No movement in any direction should be made.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=first.

text_original: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject holding (his purpose) fast as if by a (thong made from the) hide of a yellow ox, which cannot be broken.
text_normalized: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject holding (his purpose) fast as if by a (thong made from the) hide of a yellow ox, which cannot be broken.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=second.

text_original: The third NINE, undivided, shows one retiring but bound,--to his distress and peril. (If he were to deal with his binders as in) nourishing a servant or concubine, it would be fortunate for him.
text_normalized: The third NINE, undivided, shows one retiring but bound,--to his distress and peril. (If he were to deal with his binders as in) nourishing a servant or concubine, it would be fortunate for him.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=third.

text_original: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring notwithstanding his likings. In a superior man this will lead to good fortune; a small man cannot attain to this.
text_normalized: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring notwithstanding his likings. In a superior man this will lead to good fortune; a small man cannot attain to this.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fourth.

text_original: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring in an admirable way. With firm correctness there will be good fortune.
text_normalized: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring in an admirable way. With firm correctness there will be good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fifth.

text_original: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring in a noble way. It will be advantageous in every respect.
text_normalized: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring in a noble way. It will be advantageous in every respect.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=top.
